content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of size NNN, which is initially a permutation of the integers in the range [1,N][1, N][1,N].

You can do the following operation multiple times on AAA:


Choose i,ji, ji,j such that 1≤i<j≤∣A∣1 \le i < j \le |A|1≤i<j≤∣A∣ and Ai>AjA_i > A_jAi​>Aj​, then choose to delete either AiA_iAi​ or AjA_jAj​.
We say an array AAA is **terminal** if it is not possible to perform any operations on AAA. We say an array BBB is **reachable** from AAA if there exists a (possibly empty) sequence of operations that can be performed on AAA to make it equal to BBB.

Find the number of reachable terminal arrays from the initial array AAA. Since the answer may be large, print it modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line contains a single integer NNN - the size of the array.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of reachable terminal arrays modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: Ai≠AjA_i \ne A_jAi​=Aj​ for all 1≤i<j≤N1 \le i \lt j \le N1≤i<j≤N
The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : The given array is already terminal, and hence cannot lead to any other arrays. Thus, there is only one reachable terminal array, which is itself.
Test Case 2 : Do one operation choosing i=1i = 1i=1 and j=2j = 2j=2. If we choose to delete AiA_iAi​, we get the array [2][2][2] which is terminal. If we choose to delete AjA_jAj​, we get the array [1][1][1] which is terminal. Thus, there are 222 terminal arrays.
Test Case 3 : The reachable terminal arrays are [1][1][1], [2][2][2], [3][3][3] and [1,2][1, 2][1,2].
